para_trans: Regarding declaration of Reporting Authority and Reviewing Authority for Confidential Reporting of officers of the rank of Deputy District Election Officer in the cadre of Deputy Collector under the Revenue Department.
Government Circular No. Sankorn 0426 / P.No.95 / Aastha-2 (E-1642119) Madam Cama Marg, Martyrdom Rajguru Chowk,

According to Section 13-A of the Representation of the People Act, 1950, the Chief Electoral Officer is appointed for each state to perform the functions of elections under the Control of Election Commission of India. As per the provisions of the same Act, various posts have been created by the Chief Electoral Officer at the state and zonal levels for the purpose of conducting elections and electoral rolls in the state. The office of the Chief Electoral Officer is responsible for the conduct of elections in the state in a clean and fair environment. For this, a total of 1375 posts have been created in various cadres under the Department of Revenue and election related work is done regularly by the Regional Level Office under the Election Office. These mainly include revision of electoral rolls, registration of new voters, deletion of names of deceased, duplicate voters and correction of name, address, etc., voter awareness, organizing various programmes for voter awareness, conduct of Assembly and Lok Sabha by-elections, conduct of Maharashtra Legislative Council by-elections, conduct of Teachers' and Graduates' constituency elections, etc. However, the said Government decision prescribes that the increase in these posts should be construed as a temporary flood in the revenue cadre. That is, these posts are working under the command of the Revenue Department. Also, transfers / promotions of officers / staff to these posts etc. are handled by the Revenue Department.

The Deputy District Election Officer is one of the important district-level posts created by the Government decision of the Election Office and officers of the Deputy Collector cadre are appointed from the Revenue Department to that post. Confidential reports of the officers of the post are submitted to the concerned Divisional Commissioner (on MahaPAR) through the concerned District Collector online for revaluation. However, the officer holding the post of Deputy District Election Officer is only entrusted with election matters and is not entrusted with any other functions of the Revenue Department. That is, the officer is only from the electoral office.
Government Circular No: Narrow 0426 / P.No.95 / Astha-2 ($-9 & 82999)
Working under the authority, they are not entrusted with any functions other than elections.

Therefore, it will not be possible for the Additional Chief Secretary and other officers of the MARAT of the Chief Electoral Officer to jointly supervise the work of the said officers. Therefore, the confidential report of the officer for the period for which the officer holds the office of Deputy District Election Officer shall be reviewed jointly by the Additional Chief Secretary and the Chief Electoral Officer, State of Maharashtra. The government is considering taking action accordingly.

Government Circular: - The post of Deputy District Election Officer is held by officers of the cadre of Deputy Collector from the Revenue Department. However, for confidential reporting of officers of the cadre of Deputy District Electoral Officer, the respective District Collector shall be the Reporting Authority and the Additional Chief Secretary and Chief Electoral Officer, State of Maharashtra shall be the Reviewing Authority.
The reporting authority and the reviewing authority shall be the same as above when processing confidential reports from the reporting years 2025-26.
This Government Decision is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in.
